calm: Every possible book exists here, I guess.
The kiosk gives you anything. Anything at all. That's something.
Heaven better be worth all of this.
I wonder how far the chasm goes?
anxious: I'm sick of the monotony. I'm sick of having nothing to look forward to.
How long have you been here? Don't answer that. I don't want to know.
I keep opening books. I don't know why. They're all the same.
Have you noticed that we don't dream anymore?
The silence at night. I can't — I just can't anymore.
Do you hear that? No. No, of course you don't. There's nothing to hear.
mad: GOD SPOKE TO ME. He said SCOURGE THEM.
Let me see what's inside the soft body.
Kill me. Kill me and I'll come back and I'll FIND IT.
The morning HOUSED the leaves AGAIN into FOUR! DO YOU UNDERSTAND?
dead: lies still on the carpet. They'll be back at dawn.
is crumpled near the wall. Not breathing. Tomorrow.
lies face down between the shelves. Temporary.
muttering_calm: You hear someone turning pages nearby.
Quiet footsteps echo from a few galleries over.
You hear a book being slid back onto a shelf.
A voice, too far to make out the words.
Someone sighs, a few corridors away.
Faint humming drifts down the corridor.
You hear someone walking. Steady, unhurried.
muttering_anxious: Someone is pacing nearby. Back and forth.
A muttered voice, tight and rapid. You can't make out the words.
You hear someone breathing hard a few galleries over.
Restless footsteps. Someone who can't sit still.
A sound like someone hitting a shelf. Then silence.
Quiet sobbing from somewhere down the corridor.
muttering_mad: Shouting echoes from down the corridor. Incoherent.
Someone is screaming about the books. The words don't make sense.
A ranting voice, rising and falling. Getting closer? No. Staying put.
You hear tearing paper. Then laughter.
Someone is howling. The sound bounces off the shelves.
A voice is reciting numbers. Loudly. Over and over.
talk_calm: They listen. Nod slowly. “It's good to talk to someone.”
“I forget sometimes that other people are here. That I'm not the only one.”
A pause. “I keep looking for a system. Some way to narrow it down. There isn't one.”
“You know, we could be here a very long time. I try not to think about it.”
They look at you properly. “You're still trying, aren't you. I can tell.”
talk_anxious: They flinch when you speak. Then relax, slightly. “Sorry. I'm — sorry.”
“Do you think — no. Never mind. It doesn't matter.”
Rapid breathing. “I'm fine. I'm fine. Talk to me. Please.”
“I keep thinking I hear something. In the shelves. Behind me.”
They grab your arm, then let go. “Sorry. I just needed to know you were real.”
talk_mad: They stare through you. “You — YOU understand. The PATTERN.”
“The books told me. They TOLD me. You're part of it.”
Wild eyes. Words tumbling. Nothing connects to nothing.
They laugh. It goes on too long. “Sorry. What were you saying?”
“I know what you are. I KNOW.” They don't.
A torrent of words. Fragments of theology, mathematics, rage.
talk_catatonic: Nothing. Their eyes are open but they're not looking at anything.
“...” Their lips move. No sound comes out.
They blink. Once. Slowly. They don't respond.
talk_kind_player: You sit with them. You listen.
You tell them it's going to be okay. You don't know if it's true.
You speak gently. The words don't matter as much as the tone.
You ask how they're doing. A simple question. Almost unbearably so.
talk_neutral_player: You make conversation. The kind that fills space.
You exchange a few words. Nothing important. Nothing needs to be.
Small talk. The weather doesn't exist here, but you talk around it.
You acknowledge each other. That's enough, sometimes.
talk_dismissive_player: You brush them off. There's nothing they can tell you.
You turn away before they finish speaking.
You say something short. It lands hard.
You don't have time for this. You tell them so.
spend_time: You sit together in silence. The silence is different when someone is next to you.
You talk about nothing. About the shelves. About the carpet. It helps.
You share a meal from the kiosk. The food tastes the same, but eating together is different.
Time passes. For once, you don't count it.
You sit against the shelves and just exist in the same space.
Neither of you speaks much. You don't need to.
You tell them about something you remember. They tell you something back.
You lean against the wall and watch the corridor together. Nothing happens. That's fine.
recruit_accept: They look at you for a long moment. Then nod. “Okay. I'll come with you.”
A long breath. “Alright. Lead the way.”
“Better than being alone. Anything is better than being alone.”
recruit_refuse_unfamiliar: They shake their head. “I don't know you well enough for that.”
“Who are you, exactly? I'm not going anywhere with a stranger.”
“Maybe. But not yet. I need to know you better.”
recruit_refuse_affinity: They look away. “I don't think so.”
“I'd rather be alone than with someone who doesn't care.”
“No. I don't trust you enough for that.”
recruit_refuse_disposition: They're not capable of understanding what you're asking.
The words don't reach them. Whatever they're hearing, it isn't you.
They stare past you. The invitation means nothing to them.
